bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about politician Levi Boone, for 10 points each.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about politician Levi Boone, for 10 points each.
parts: Boone served as mayor of this city whose police department fired at protestors on Clark Street Bridge. Higher liquor license costs led to that Lager Beer Riot in this city, where later violence occurred during the Haymarket Riot.
Boone won the Chicago mayoral election with this party and advocated for temperance. This political party unsuccessfully fielded Millard Fillmore as a presidential candidate in 1856.

Levi Boone’s Irish successor Thomas Dyer founded this institution that now occupies a building topped with a statue of Ceres. This institution created a grading scheme for one commodity that ranged from “Club” to “Rejected.”
parts_sanitized: Boone served as mayor of this city whose police department fired at protestors on Clark Street Bridge. Higher liquor license costs led to that Lager Beer Riot in this city, where later violence occurred during the Haymarket Riot.
Boone won the Chicago mayoral election with this party and advocated for temperance. This political party unsuccessfully fielded Millard Fillmore as a presidential candidate in 1856.

Levi Boone's Irish successor Thomas Dyer founded this institution that now occupies a building topped with a statue of Ceres. This institution created a grading scheme for one commodity that ranged from "Club" to "Rejected."
answers: Chicago Board of Trade [or CBT; prompt on partial answers] (That commodity is wheat.)
answers_sanitized: Know-Nothing Party [accept American Party or Native American Party]
Chicago Board of Trade [or CBT; prompt on partial answers] (That commodity is wheat.)
